text.title: CSS-in-JS Basics Anti-patterns
text.summary: Common failure modes: styled definitions inside render, non-transient props, missing SSR registry, skipped DefaultTheme augmentation.

antipattern.description: Defining a styled.X component inside a React function body.
antipattern.reason: Every render creates a new component class with a new generated className, causing re-mounts and style thrashing on every state change. Move styled components to module scope.

antipattern.description: Using non-transient prop names (variant, size, color) in styled-components without the $ prefix.
antipattern.reason: React forwards unknown props to the DOM, producing "Received `true` for a non-boolean attribute" warnings and invalid HTML. Requires shouldForwardProp or the $ prefix.
Without augmentation, theme is typed any inside template literals; agents inline hex values instead of consuming token variables, breaking the design contract.

antipattern.description: Copying Pages Router SSR setup (_document.tsx) into Next.js App Router.
antipattern.reason: App Router does not use _document.tsx; styled-components in App Router requires a 'use client' registry component wrapping the tree, not a custom Document.

antipattern.description: Adding a styled component to a library published to npm.
antipattern.reason: Forces consumers to install and bundle the runtime. Published component libraries should use Emotion's css prop with the Babel plugin, vanilla-extract, or plain CSS Modules.
